input.documentary_description: A 60-minute documentary about three independent bookshop owners fighting to keep their stores open in a city where rents have tripled in a decade.
subjects_and_scenes: Elena Park: bookshop owner, downtown. Interview + filming in her shop + her landlord negotiation meeting
James Obi: bookshop owner, university district. Interview + shop footage + community reading event he hosts
Margaret Liu: bookshop owner, waterfront. Interview + shop footage + her packing up unsold stock
City council representative: 20-minute interview at city hall about commercial rent policy
B-roll: city streets showing chain stores replacing independents, empty storefronts, gentrification context shots
Opening sequence: time-lapse of a book being printed, shipped, shelved, and sold (requires setup)
input.constraints: Elena's landlord meeting is only on Wednesday; James's reading event is Thursday evening
input.crew: Director, Camera Operator, Sound Recordist. Single camera.
input.notes: All locations in the same city, no overnight stays needed

input.documentary_description: A short documentary about a pottery workshop
subjects_and_scenes: Interview with the potter; footage of the workshop; finished pieces

input.documentary_description: A 52-minute documentary about the revival of night trains in Europe, following passengers and staff on the Vienna-to-Rome overnight service.
subjects_and_scenes: Vienna Hauptbahnhof: establishing shots of the station, boarding sequence, departure (must be filmed in the evening, train departs at 19:40)
On-board filming: overnight sequences with passengers and crew in sleeping cars (requires special access from ÖBB/Nightjet)
Arrival in Rome Termini: morning arrival, passengers disembarking, station atmosphere
Interview with ÖBB Nightjet operations manager — Vienna office, 45-minute sit-down
Interview with a regular passenger (commutes monthly) — on-board or at their Vienna home
Interview with Italian railway historian — Rome, discussing the history of European sleeper services
B-roll: Vienna and Rome cityscapes for context, train details (livery, beds, dining car)
input.constraints: Train runs nightly but access permission from ÖBB is confirmed for two specific nights only; Rome historian available only on Friday; ÖBB manager available Monday or Tuesday
input.crew: Director, Camera Operator, Sound Recordist, Production Coordinator (4-person crew)
input.notes: Crew flies Vienna to Rome on the train itself (one of the two permitted nights); second permitted night is backup
